all_violations.description: Thread.Sleep(50) inside synchronous file-IO retry helper TryWriteFaultFile blocks the test thread.
all_violations.suggestion: Convert TryWriteFaultFile to async and replace Thread.Sleep with await Task.Delay inside the bounded retry loop.

all_violations.description: Thread.Sleep(50) inside synchronous file-IO retry helper TryDeleteFaultFile blocks the test thread.
all_violations.suggestion: Convert TryDeleteFaultFile to async and replace Thread.Sleep with await Task.Delay inside the bounded retry loop.

all_violations.description: PERSISTENT REGRESSION (flagged 2026-04-18): Task.Delay(17s) used as a real-time wait for hosted-service capture instead of a fake clock.
all_violations.suggestion: Inject TimeProvider into DaprHealthHistoryCollector and use FakeTimeProvider.Advance(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(15)) to drive the timer deterministically.

all_violations.description: PERSISTENT REGRESSION (flagged 2026-04-18): Task.Delay(17s) waits on hosted-service tick in ExecuteAsync_SkipsWrite_WhenNoComponentsReturned.
all_violations.suggestion: Replace with FakeTimeProvider.Advance and a TaskCompletionSource signal from the collector when an iteration completes.

all_violations.description: PERSISTENT REGRESSION (flagged 2026-04-18): Task.Delay(17s) waits on hosted-service tick in ExecuteAsync_ContinuesOnWriteFailure.
all_violations.suggestion: Replace with FakeTimeProvider.Advance and a poll-with-deadline that observes infraService.Received().GetComponentsAsync.

all_violations.description: Task.Delay(2000) outside a polling loop while waiting for sidecar actor placement registration.
all_violations.suggestion: Replace with a bounded poll loop against a sidecar readiness endpoint (e.g. /v1.0/metadata or actor placement health) with a deadline.

all_violations.description: Task.Delay(2000) outside a polling loop while waiting for sidecar actor placement registration in Tenants integration fixture.
all_violations.suggestion: Replace with a bounded poll loop against the Dapr actor placement readiness endpoint with a deadline.

all_violations.description: Asserts ProcessedAt against DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddSeconds(-5) without an injected clock.
all_violations.suggestion: Inject TimeProvider into IdempotencyRecord.FromResult and assert against a fixed FakeTimeProvider.GetUtcNow() value.

all_violations.description: Asserts FailedAt > DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddMinutes(-1) using wall-clock comparison.
all_violations.suggestion: Inject TimeProvider into the dead-letter recording path and assert FailedAt equals a fixed FakeTimeProvider value.

all_violations.description: Asserts FailedAt <= DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddSeconds(1); flaky under GC pause or slow CI agent.
all_violations.suggestion: Use FakeTimeProvider so the recorded FailedAt equals a known fixed instant; replace the inequality with ShouldBe.

all_violations.description: Task.Delay(100) outside a polling loop while waiting for hosted-service to evaluate disabled flag.
all_violations.suggestion: Replace with a TaskCompletionSource signaled by the collector or a poll-with-deadline asserting infraService.DidNotReceive().

all_violations.description: FakeEventPublisher, FakeDeadLetterPublisher, InMemoryCommandStatusStore, and FakeDomainServiceInvoker are owned by the collection-scoped fixture and accumulate state across every test in the [Collection("DaprTestContainer")]; there is no per-test Reset() invoked between tests, so accumulated topics, published events, and command status records leak across tests within the same fixture lifetime.
all_violations.suggestion: Add a Reset() helper on the fixture (and on FakeEventPublisher/FakeDeadLetterPublisher/InMemoryCommandStatusStore) and invoke it from each test class constructor or via an IDisposable per-test wrapper that runs before each test.

all_violations.description: A static Lazy<Task<IConnectionMultiplexer>> RedisConnection is initialized once per AppDomain and never disposed; the multiplexer leaks across tests, classes, and even fixture lifecycles, and any state cached in Redis between Tier 2 runs is observable to subsequent tests.
all_violations.suggestion: Move the multiplexer into the DaprTestContainerFixture (IAsyncLifetime) so it is created/disposed with the collection, or wrap each access in a using block; flush the relevant Redis keys in DisposeAsync.

all_violations.description: Constructor and Dispose both null-out EVENTSTORE_ADMIN_URL/TOKEN/FORMAT without first capturing the prior process value; if the developer or CI sets these env vars before invoking dotnet test, those values are silently destroyed for the rest of the process lifetime.
all_violations.suggestion: Mirror the snapshot/restore pattern used in GlobalOptionsBindingProfileTests.cs: capture _origUrl/_origToken/_origFormat in the constructor and write them back in Dispose.

all_violations.description: InitializeAsync mutates EnableKeycloak / ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT / DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT before BuildAsync/StartAsync; if any of those steps throws, xUnit does NOT call DisposeAsync (per xUnit semantics), so the env vars leak into any downstream collection executed in the same process.
all_violations.suggestion: Wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch (mirroring AspirePubSubProofTestFixture), restore the env-var snapshot in the catch block, then rethrow.

all_violations.description: Same xUnit IAsyncLifetime hazard: 4 env vars (EnableKeycloak, ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT, DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT, EventStore__SampleFaults__MalformedProjectResponse) are mutated before the topology starts; failure during BuildAsync/StartAsync skips DisposeAsync and leaks them.
all_violations.suggestion: Adopt AspirePubSubProofTestFixture's SnapshotAndSet/RestoreEnvironmentSnapshot helpers and wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch for guaranteed restoration.
all_violations.code_snippet:
Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable("EventStore__SampleFaults__MalformedProjectResponse", "true");
... // No try/catch around BuildAsync/StartAsync

all_violations.description: Three env vars (EnableKeycloak, ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT, DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT) are mutated before BuildAsync/StartAsync; Keycloak realm import in particular often fails on slow CI hardware, and that failure path leaks the env vars because xUnit skips DisposeAsync.
all_violations.suggestion: Adopt the SnapshotAndSet/RestoreEnvironmentSnapshot pattern and a try/catch around InitializeAsync.
all_violations.code_snippet:
Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable("EnableKeycloak", "true");
... // 5-min Keycloak startup may throw; DisposeAsync never runs

all_violations.description: Mutates EnableKeycloak before a 5-minute Keycloak startup; if WaitForEndpointAsync, WaitForTokenAcquisitionAsync, or any health probe throws, xUnit does not call DisposeAsync and EnableKeycloak=true leaks to subsequent collections (potentially forcing them to start unwanted Keycloak containers).
all_violations.suggestion: Wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch and restore EnableKeycloak in the catch path before rethrowing.
all_violations.code_snippet:
_previousEnableKeycloak = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("EnableKeycloak");
Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable("EnableKeycloak", "true");

all_violations.description: DAPR_HTTP_PORT/DAPR_GRPC_PORT are set after capture, but the prerequisite/start-host/sidecar startup that follows can throw (Redis unreachable, daprd path missing, port conflict); on failure xUnit skips DisposeAsync and the in-process Dapr Actors runtime resolves to the abandoned ports for the rest of the test run.
all_violations.suggestion: Wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch and explicitly restore the two DAPR_* env vars before rethrowing.

all_violations.description: Same hazard as the EventStore DAPR fixture: DAPR_HTTP_PORT/DAPR_GRPC_PORT are mutated before VerifyPrerequisitesAsync/StartTestHostAsync/StartDaprSidecar; a throw from any of those skips DisposeAsync.
all_violations.suggestion: Wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch with explicit env-var restoration before rethrow.
all_violations.code_snippet:
_previousDaprHttpPort = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("DAPR_HTTP_PORT");
Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable("DAPR_HTTP_PORT", _daprHttpPort.ToString());

all_violations.description: Test creates HttpClient via _factory.CreateClient() but never disposes it; the WebApplicationFactory tracks created clients, but the test class itself is not IDisposable so handles leak across the class fixture lifetime.
all_violations.suggestion: Implement IDisposable on the test class and dispose the HttpClient in Dispose, or use a using statement.
all_violations.code_snippet:
HttpClient client = _factory.CreateClient();
// No using, no Dispose

all_violations.description: [CollectionDefinition("SignalRRedisBackplaneProofTests", DisableParallelization = true)] disables parallelism without an inline justification comment; the rationale (Redis backplane port-isolation under concurrent runs) lives only in the test file at line 412 and is easy to lose during refactor.
all_violations.suggestion: Add a /// <summary> comment on the collection class explaining the Redis-backplane parallelism hazard, mirroring the docstrings on AspireTopologyCollection.

all_violations.description: LogCapturingFactory.LogProvider accumulates entries across tests in the class fixture; tests rely on calling _factory.LogProvider.Clear() at the top of each test to avoid bleed-over. xUnit serializes tests in the same class fixture, so this works, but is fragile if [Theory]/parallel collection is added.
all_violations.suggestion: Either expose a per-test scope (IDisposable that wraps LogProvider) or migrate to a per-test factory; at minimum add a comment that any test added must invoke Clear() first.

all_violations.description: File is 1,455 LOC (4.85x the 300 LOC DoD limit) with 81 [Fact]/[Theory] methods spanning Tenant lifecycle, user membership, RBAC, role escalation, and state replay. The single-class structure makes navigation, code review, and merge-conflict resolution painful.
all_violations.suggestion: Split into TenantLifecycleTests, TenantUserMembershipTests, TenantRbacTests, and TenantStateReplayTests by domain area; each file should stay under 300 LOC.
all_violations.code_snippet: 1,455 LOC, 81 [Fact]/[Theory] — largest test file in the suite

all_violations.description: File is 1,169 LOC (3.9x the 300 LOC limit) with 54 [Fact]/[Theory] methods covering compliance assertions, conformance suite expectations, and aggregate behavior validation.
all_violations.suggestion: Decompose by conformance dimension (lifecycle, RBAC, persistence, multi-tenancy) into separate files.
all_violations.code_snippet: 1,169 LOC, 54 test methods, includes a private AssertEventsEqual helper at line 1143

all_violations.description: File is 1,109 LOC (3.7x the limit) with 50 [Fact]/[Theory] methods. Has grown by 79 LOC since the 2026-04-18 review (1,030 -> 1,109), confirming the file is on a runaway trajectory.
all_violations.suggestion: Split into ProcessAsync_PayloadDispatch, ProcessAsync_StateRehydration, ProcessAsync_RecordAggregates, and ProcessAsync_TerminationSemantics test classes.
all_violations.code_snippet: Grew from 1,030 to 1,109 LOC since 2026-04-18 — DoD violation worsening, not stabilising

all_violations.description: File is 934 LOC (3.1x the limit) with 22 test methods, four of which exceed the 80 LOC method-size threshold (89, 83, 104, 94 LOC) — combined file-size + method-size DoD breach.
all_violations.suggestion: Extract TrackIdentityAsync_* tests into ProjectionCheckpointTrackerIdentityTrackingTests; keep checkpoint persistence tests in the original file.
all_violations.code_snippet: Methods >80 LOC: TrackIdentityAsync_RetryAfterPriorFailure (89), TrackIdentityAsync_FullIdentityPage_AppendsToNextPageAndUpdatesIndex (94), TrackIdentityAsync_AggregateIdentityNormalization (83), TrackIdentityAsync_MixedAndLowerCaseInputs (104)

all_violations.description: File is 813 LOC (2.7x the limit). Contains the StateManager reflection duplication anti-pattern.
all_violations.suggestion: Split by trace-chain stage (origin capture, propagation, consumer) and adopt the existing AggregateActorTestHelper.AttachStateManager pattern.
all_violations.code_snippet: 813 LOC + reflection duplication at line 81

all_violations.description: File is 808 LOC (2.7x the limit) with two distinct StateManager reflection sites (line 43, line 63) that should call into the existing AggregateActorTestHelper.
all_violations.suggestion: Split by recovery phase (drain start, retry, completion); centralise StateManager reflection.
all_violations.code_snippet: 808 LOC + 2 reflection sites at lines 43, 63

all_violations.description: File is 793 LOC (2.6x limit). Contains the only test method exceeding 120 LOC in the suite (QueryCacheTopology_WhenProjectionChanges_InvalidatesOldETagAndCachesNewETag, 121 LOC) AND three private Assert* helper methods that hide ShouldBeTrue/ShouldThrow assertions inside named helpers.
all_violations.suggestion: Split into per-scenario classes; rename Assert* helpers to neutral names (e.g. WarmCacheNotModifiedScenario) that signal they perform multi-step orchestration, not just an assertion.
all_violations.code_snippet: Hidden assertions at lines 349 (AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync), 391 (AssertExpectedCountETagContract), 496 (AssertPersistedEventEvidence), 508 (AssertStrongSelfRoutingETag)

all_violations.description: File is 771 LOC (2.6x limit) with 32 [Fact]/[Theory] methods; representative of unsplit controller test files across the suite.
all_violations.suggestion: Split by HTTP verb / endpoint: QueriesController_GetTests, QueriesController_PostTests, QueriesController_AuthorizationTests.
all_violations.code_snippet: 771 LOC, 32 test methods

all_violations.description: File is 753 LOC (2.5x limit). Has a `// Story 11-3 Task 6:` story-number comment at line 29 that will rot relative to commit history per CLAUDE.md guidance.
all_violations.suggestion: Split by orchestration concern; remove story-number annotations.
all_violations.code_snippet: 753 LOC + story-number rot at line 29

all_violations.description: File is 709 LOC (2.4x limit). Final entry in the >700 LOC DoD-breaching cohort.
all_violations.suggestion: Split by cache scenario (hit, miss, eviction, invalidation).
all_violations.code_snippet: 709 LOC

all_violations.description: An additional 80 test files exceed the 300 LOC DoD limit beyond the top 10 itemised above — total 90 violating files across the suite (632 test files total, ~14% violate DoD). The pattern is systemic, not isolated. Examples include AuthorizationBehaviorTests (676), DeadLettersPageTests (666), EventStoreSignalRClientTests (648), AccessControlPolicyTests (640), DeadLetterTraceChainTests (631), AssemblyScannerTests (621), TenantsPageTests (608), EventPublisherTests (601), AggregateActorDomainResultTests (583), DaprTestContainerFixture (562), PersistThenPublishResilienceTests (560), NamingConventionEngineTests (559).
all_violations.suggestion: Treat the 300 LOC limit as a hard pre-merge check, not advisory. Add a CI guard that fails when any file under tests/ or Hexalith.Tenants/tests/ exceeds 300 LOC.
all_violations.code_snippet: 90 of 632 test files (~14%) violate the 300 LOC DoD; cohort spans 309-1455 LOC

all_violations.description: The pattern `typeof(Actor).GetProperty("StateManager", BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance)` followed by `prop?.SetValue(actor, stateManager)` is duplicated across 24 test files (32 occurrences) despite an existing AggregateActorTestHelper that already encapsulates the pattern at line 75. The helper exists; tests do not call it.
all_violations.suggestion: Extract a public static `ActorTestHarness.AttachStateManager<TActor>(TActor actor, IActorStateManager mock)` and migrate all 32 sites. Estimated ~96 LOC eliminated (3 LOC per site × 32 sites) and a single point of failure if Dapr renames the property.
all_violations.code_snippet: Files: Telemetry/EndToEndTraceTests.cs:52; Observability/DeadLetterTraceChainTests.cs:77,138,191; Observability/DeadLetterOriginTracingTests.cs:81; Observability/DeadLetterMessageCompletenessTests.cs:72; Security/TenantInjectionPreventionTests.cs:74,112,152; Events/AtLeastOnceDeliveryTests.cs:268; Security/SecurityAuditLoggingTests.cs:142; Events/PersistThenPublishResilienceTests.cs:66; Integration/ETagActorIntegrationTests.cs:328; Security/DataPathIsolationTests.cs:144,203,254; Actors/StateMachineIntegrationTests.cs:60; Actors/MultiTenantPublicationTests.cs:55,127,169,217; Actors/EventReplayProjectionActorTests.cs:37; Actors/EventPublicationIntegrationTests.cs:57; Actors/EventDrainRecoveryTests.cs:43,63; Actors/ETagActorTests.cs:35; Actors/DeadLetterRoutingTests.cs:66; Actors/BackpressureTests.cs:58; Actors/AggregateActorGetEventsTests.cs:49

all_violations.description: Five private reflection helpers (InvokeProjectionChanged at 587, InvokeOnReconnectedAsync at 595, InvokeOnClosedAsync at 606, plus the ReconnectConfigurator nested-type accessor at 620 and the private constructor invocation at 626) reach into private members of EventStoreSignalRClient. The repeated GetMethod(..., BindingFlags.NonPublic) pattern indicates the SUT exposes too little public API for testability.
all_violations.suggestion: Refactor EventStoreSignalRClient to expose protected/internal hooks (e.g. via [InternalsVisibleTo] or a TestHook seam) so tests do not need 5 reflection sites.
all_violations.code_snippet: Lines 587-635: 5 separate reflection accessors for OnProjectionChanged, OnReconnectedAsync, OnClosedAsync, ReconnectConfigurator nested type, and a private constructor

all_violations.description: Helpers named `Assert*` perform multi-step orchestration that includes assertions but also state mutations and request issuance. Naming a helper Assert* implies a single-purpose check; readers expecting that surface are misled. Same anti-pattern present in another 14 files (DaprTestContainerFixture, AccessControlPolicyTests, PayloadProtectionTests, SignalRRedisBackplaneRuntimeProofTests, ValidProjectionRoundTripE2ETests, OpenApiE2ETests, HttpStaleETagProofE2ETests, ResiliencyConfigurationTests, ProblemTypeUriComplianceTests, EventDrainRecoveryTests, PubSubTopicIsolationEnforcementTests, TenantsDaprTestFixture, AspireTopologyFixture, TenantConformanceTests).
all_violations.suggestion: Rename helpers to action-oriented verbs (e.g. AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync -> EnsureWarmRequestReturnsNotModifiedAndCaptureETag); keep pure assertions narrowly scoped.
all_violations.code_snippet: private async Task AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync(QueryIdentity query, string baselineETag, string firstCorrelationId, ...) — issues HTTP requests, captures ETags, and asserts; not a pure check

all_violations.description: TenantAggregateTests uses `snake_case_with_underscores_throughout` style (e.g. `CreateTenant_with_no_prior_state_produces_TenantCreated`) — 59 of 81 test methods follow the underscore-rich `Method_with_scenario_words_produces_outcome` style instead of the codebase-standard `Method_Scenario_Expected` Pascal-style scenario seen in EventStoreAggregateTests.cs (45/50 matching) and ProjectionCheckpointTrackerTests.cs (21/22 matching). Reviewer-team consistency suffers.
all_violations.suggestion: Choose one convention project-wide (Method_Scenario_Expected matches the .NET community norm) and refactor TenantAggregateTests to match. Document the convention in CLAUDE.md.
all_violations.code_snippet: Sample naming in TenantAggregateTests (snake-style): CreateTenant_with_no_prior_state_produces_TenantCreated, AddUserToTenant_when_user_already_member_produces_UserAlreadyInTenantRejection, ChangeUserRole_with_undefined_role_produces_RoleEscalationRejection. Sample in EventStoreAggregateTests (Pascal-scenario): ProcessAsync_MatchingHandleMethod_DispatchesCorrectly, ProcessAsync_NullState_PassesNullToHandle

all_violations.description: 12 test methods exceed the 80 LOC threshold across the suite. Worst offender: QueryCacheTopology_WhenProjectionChanges_InvalidatesOldETagAndCachesNewETag at 121 LOC. Other notable offenders: PostCommands_SignedHappyPath_ReachesCompletedWithEvent (112), RedisBackplane_TwoEventStoreInstances_DeliversProjectionChangedAcrossInstances (110), ProcessCommand_CrashAtEventsStored_Resume_DoesNotRePersistEvents (102), TrackIdentityAsync_MixedAndLowerCaseInputsForSameAggregate_ConvergeOnSamePersistedIdentity (104), TrackIdentityAsync_FullIdentityPage_AppendsToNextPageAndUpdatesIndex (94), TenantBootstrap_FirstSixtySeconds_NoFailureEvents (96), TrackIdentityAsync_RetryAfterPriorFailure_CompletesRegistrationOnSecondCall (89), TrackIdentityAsync_AggregateIdentityNormalization_FlowsCanonicalLowercaseToTrackerPersistence (83), StateMachineIntegrationTests:ProcessCommand_CrashAtEventsStored_Resume_PreservesResultPayload (86).
all_violations.suggestion: Extract Arrange-heavy logic into private builders or shared fixtures; aim to keep test bodies under 40 LOC.
all_violations.code_snippet: Single-Fact 121-LOC method; readability and review cost grows superlinearly with length

all_violations.description: 10 test classes exceed 30 [Fact]/[Theory] methods, hindering code review and IDE navigation: TenantAggregateTests (81), TenantConformanceTests (54), NamingConventionEngineTests (51), EventStoreAggregateTests (50), AssemblyScannerTests (44), AggregateIdentityTests (38), EventStoreSignalRClientTests (35), MessageTypeTests (35), SnapshotManagerTests (32), QueriesControllerTests (32).
all_violations.suggestion: Apply the same domain-area-split rule as for oversized files; aim for <20 [Fact]/[Theory] methods per class.
all_violations.code_snippet: 10 classes >30 test methods; top class has 81

all_violations.description: Story/Epic identifiers are embedded in 88 test files (164 occurrences total). Examples: NamingConventionEngineTests:447 `// Story 18-4: GetQueryTypeName tests (Task 8)`, OpenTelemetryRegistrationTests:10 `/// Story 6.1 Task 7: OpenTelemetry registration tests.`, DeadLetterOriginTracingTests:37 `/// Story 6.3: Dead-letter-to-origin tracing tests.`, ProjectionUpdateOrchestratorTests:29 `/// Story 11-3 Task 6: ProjectionUpdateOrchestrator tests.`. These references rot relative to commit history (story IDs change between sprints; tasks renumber).
all_violations.suggestion: Strip story/epic/task identifiers from XML doc-comments and inline comments. Keep semantic descriptions of WHY the test exists, not the planning artifact.
all_violations.code_snippet: 164 Story/Epic comment occurrences across 88 distinct test files

all_violations.description: Lines 411, 429, 444, 459 are 4 near-identical [Fact] tests that differ only in the JSON payload string — perfect [Theory] candidates with [InlineData]. Same pattern repeats around lines 332, 342, 356 (state-rehydration variants). These could collapse to ~2 Theory methods reducing ~80 LOC.
all_violations.suggestion: Convert ProcessAsync_JsonElementArray_With{UnknownEventType,NonObjectEntry,MissingEventTypeName,InvalidPayloadShape} into a single [Theory] with [InlineData] for each invalid JSON shape.

all_violations.description: DomainResultAssertionsTests.cs mixes 4 xUnit Assert.* calls with 8 Shouldly Should* calls within the same file. The Hexalith.EventStore.Client.Tests/Aggregates/EventStoreAggregateTests.cs is xUnit-only (98 Assert.*, 0 Shouldly) while CLAUDE.md prescribes Shouldly fluent assertions — broader inconsistency exists between projects (Sample.Tests, Testing.Tests use Shouldly; Client.Tests, Server.Tests largely use xUnit Assert).
all_violations.suggestion: Adopt CLAUDE.md's stated standard (Shouldly) project-wide; add an .editorconfig analyzer rule banning xUnit Assert.Equal/True/False/NotNull in tests.
all_violations.code_snippet: DomainResultAssertionsTests: 4 xUnit Assert vs 8 Shouldly Should* in one file. Cross-project: EventStoreAggregateTests is 100% xUnit (98 Assert.*); GreetingAggregateTests is 100% Shouldly.

all_violations.description: Test traits ([Trait("Tier", "...")] / [Trait("Category", "...")]) are present in 36 of 581 .NET test files in tests/ (6.2% coverage). Sub-project breakdown: Server.Tests has 5 of 169 files trait-tagged (3.0%); Admin.Server.Tests has 3 of 58 (5.2%); IntegrationTests has 27 of 68 (39.7%). Selective trait usage means Tier/Category-based test selection in CI is unreliable.
all_violations.suggestion: Either trait-tag every test class in projects that use traits OR drop traits entirely. CLAUDE.md tier model (1/2/3) implies traits should be universal in IntegrationTests at minimum.
all_violations.code_snippet: Server.Tests trait coverage 5/169; IntegrationTests trait coverage 27/68; same project, inconsistent practice

all_violations.description: Tier 1 unit test sleeps Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17)) to wait for hosted-service initial-delay timer; persistent regression since 2026-04-18 review.
all_violations.suggestion: Refactor DaprHealthHistoryCollector to accept an injectable TimeProvider (or ISystemClock / Func<TimeSpan,Task> delay) so tests can advance virtual time via FakeTimeProvider — eliminate the 17s wall-clock wait entirely.
all_violations.code_snippet: await collector.StartAsync(cts.Token); await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17)); // 15s delay + 2s buffer

all_violations.description: Second 17-second Task.Delay in same Tier 1 file (ExecuteAsync_SkipsWrite_WhenNoComponentsReturned).
all_violations.suggestion: Same TimeProvider injection as line 71 — or convert to a deterministic CancellationToken-driven trigger inside the collector that the test can fire synchronously.
all_violations.code_snippet: await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17));

all_violations.description: Third 17-second Task.Delay in same Tier 1 file (ExecuteAsync_ContinuesOnWriteFailure). Combined cost: 3 x 17s = 51s of CI time per Tier 1 run for one collector class.
all_violations.suggestion: All three tests should share a TimeProvider abstraction; use Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider to advance time without sleeping.
all_violations.code_snippet: await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17));

all_violations.description: Nine IntegrationTests classes (CommandsControllerTests, CommandRoutingIntegrationTests, CommandStatusIntegrationTests, ConcurrencyConflictIntegrationTests, MultiTenantRoutingIntegrationTests, OpenApiIntegrationTests, ReplayIntegrationTests, ServiceUnavailableIntegrationTests, ValidationTests) use IClassFixture<JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory>, so each spins its own WebApplicationFactory instead of sharing one via ICollectionFixture.
all_violations.suggestion: Introduce [CollectionDefinition("JwtAuthFactory")] : ICollectionFixture<JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory> and decorate the 9 classes with [Collection("JwtAuthFactory")] — one factory boot serves all 9 classes.
all_violations.code_snippet: public class CommandsControllerTests : IClassFixture<JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory>

all_violations.description: Status-poll loop uses Task.Delay(500ms) — 500ms increments inside a Tier 3 polling loop is coarser than the 250ms used elsewhere in IntegrationTests; tightening would shorten happy-path latency.
all_violations.suggestion: Reduce to 250ms (or use the s_statusPollInterval constant pattern adopted by sibling files like CommandLifecycleTests).
all_violations.code_snippet: await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500));

all_violations.description: Many near-identical Facts of the form Local*AccessControlYaml_IsValidYaml_ParsesCorrectly / Production*AccessControlYaml_IsValidYaml_ParsesCorrectly mirror the same shape across 6+ environment+component pairs.
all_violations.suggestion: Convert to [Theory]/[InlineData("Local", "event-store")] / [InlineData("Production", "event-store")] etc. Cuts test-launch overhead and reduces 22 method signatures to 1.

all_violations.description: 22 [Fact] methods, zero [Theory]/[InlineData] in a 934-line file — multiplies xUnit per-test launch overhead.
all_violations.suggestion: Audit for parameterizable groups (e.g., checkpoint count thresholds, retry counts, timeouts) and consolidate via [Theory]/[InlineData].

all_violations.description: Thread.Sleep(50) inside file-write retry loop — minor performance impact (~250ms worst case across 5 attempts) but blocks the test thread; flagged in determinism review for synchronous-blocking concern.
all_violations.suggestion: Replace with await Task.Delay(50) and convert TryWriteFaultFile to async; also bound retry budget instead of fixed iteration count.
all_violations.code_snippet: Thread.Sleep(50);

all_violations.description: Second Thread.Sleep(50) in TryDeleteFaultFile retry loop — same characteristics as line 244.
all_violations.suggestion: Same as line 244 — convert to async Task.Delay.

all_violations.description: 25+ [Fact] methods following AuthorizationBehavior_<scenario>_<expectation> naming with shared arrange shape; some logically parametrizable (matching/wrong/missing claim variations).

high_severity_violations.suggestion: Replace with FakeTimeProvider.Advance and a TaskCompletionSource signal from the collector when an iteration completes.

high_severity_violations.description: PERSISTENT REGRESSION (flagged 2026-04-18): Task.Delay(17s) waits on hosted-service tick in ExecuteAsync_ContinuesOnWriteFailure.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Replace with FakeTimeProvider.Advance and a poll-with-deadline that observes infraService.Received().GetComponentsAsync.

high_severity_violations.description: Task.Delay(2000) outside a polling loop while waiting for sidecar actor placement registration.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Replace with a bounded poll loop against a sidecar readiness endpoint (e.g. /v1.0/metadata or actor placement health) with a deadline.

high_severity_violations.description: Task.Delay(2000) outside a polling loop while waiting for sidecar actor placement registration in Tenants integration fixture.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Replace with a bounded poll loop against the Dapr actor placement readiness endpoint with a deadline.

high_severity_violations.description: Asserts ProcessedAt against DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddSeconds(-5) without an injected clock.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Inject TimeProvider into IdempotencyRecord.FromResult and assert against a fixed FakeTimeProvider.GetUtcNow() value.

high_severity_violations.description: Asserts FailedAt > DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddMinutes(-1) using wall-clock comparison.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Inject TimeProvider into the dead-letter recording path and assert FailedAt equals a fixed FakeTimeProvider value.

high_severity_violations.description: Asserts FailedAt <= DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddSeconds(1); flaky under GC pause or slow CI agent.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Use FakeTimeProvider so the recorded FailedAt equals a known fixed instant; replace the inequality with ShouldBe.

high_severity_violations.description: FakeEventPublisher, FakeDeadLetterPublisher, InMemoryCommandStatusStore, and FakeDomainServiceInvoker are owned by the collection-scoped fixture and accumulate state across every test in the [Collection("DaprTestContainer")]; there is no per-test Reset() invoked between tests, so accumulated topics, published events, and command status records leak across tests within the same fixture lifetime.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Add a Reset() helper on the fixture (and on FakeEventPublisher/FakeDeadLetterPublisher/InMemoryCommandStatusStore) and invoke it from each test class constructor or via an IDisposable per-test wrapper that runs before each test.

high_severity_violations.description: A static Lazy<Task<IConnectionMultiplexer>> RedisConnection is initialized once per AppDomain and never disposed; the multiplexer leaks across tests, classes, and even fixture lifecycles, and any state cached in Redis between Tier 2 runs is observable to subsequent tests.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Move the multiplexer into the DaprTestContainerFixture (IAsyncLifetime) so it is created/disposed with the collection, or wrap each access in a using block; flush the relevant Redis keys in DisposeAsync.

high_severity_violations.description: File is 1,455 LOC (4.85x the 300 LOC DoD limit) with 81 [Fact]/[Theory] methods spanning Tenant lifecycle, user membership, RBAC, role escalation, and state replay. The single-class structure makes navigation, code review, and merge-conflict resolution painful.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split into TenantLifecycleTests, TenantUserMembershipTests, TenantRbacTests, and TenantStateReplayTests by domain area; each file should stay under 300 LOC.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: 1,455 LOC, 81 [Fact]/[Theory] — largest test file in the suite

high_severity_violations.description: File is 1,169 LOC (3.9x the 300 LOC limit) with 54 [Fact]/[Theory] methods covering compliance assertions, conformance suite expectations, and aggregate behavior validation.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Decompose by conformance dimension (lifecycle, RBAC, persistence, multi-tenancy) into separate files.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: 1,169 LOC, 54 test methods, includes a private AssertEventsEqual helper at line 1143

high_severity_violations.description: File is 1,109 LOC (3.7x the limit) with 50 [Fact]/[Theory] methods. Has grown by 79 LOC since the 2026-04-18 review (1,030 -> 1,109), confirming the file is on a runaway trajectory.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split into ProcessAsync_PayloadDispatch, ProcessAsync_StateRehydration, ProcessAsync_RecordAggregates, and ProcessAsync_TerminationSemantics test classes.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: Grew from 1,030 to 1,109 LOC since 2026-04-18 — DoD violation worsening, not stabilising

high_severity_violations.description: File is 934 LOC (3.1x the limit) with 22 test methods, four of which exceed the 80 LOC method-size threshold (89, 83, 104, 94 LOC) — combined file-size + method-size DoD breach.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Extract TrackIdentityAsync_* tests into ProjectionCheckpointTrackerIdentityTrackingTests; keep checkpoint persistence tests in the original file.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: Methods >80 LOC: TrackIdentityAsync_RetryAfterPriorFailure (89), TrackIdentityAsync_FullIdentityPage_AppendsToNextPageAndUpdatesIndex (94), TrackIdentityAsync_AggregateIdentityNormalization (83), TrackIdentityAsync_MixedAndLowerCaseInputs (104)

high_severity_violations.description: File is 813 LOC (2.7x the limit). Contains the StateManager reflection duplication anti-pattern.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split by trace-chain stage (origin capture, propagation, consumer) and adopt the existing AggregateActorTestHelper.AttachStateManager pattern.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: 813 LOC + reflection duplication at line 81

high_severity_violations.description: File is 808 LOC (2.7x the limit) with two distinct StateManager reflection sites (line 43, line 63) that should call into the existing AggregateActorTestHelper.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split by recovery phase (drain start, retry, completion); centralise StateManager reflection.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: 808 LOC + 2 reflection sites at lines 43, 63

high_severity_violations.description: File is 793 LOC (2.6x limit). Contains the only test method exceeding 120 LOC in the suite (QueryCacheTopology_WhenProjectionChanges_InvalidatesOldETagAndCachesNewETag, 121 LOC) AND three private Assert* helper methods that hide ShouldBeTrue/ShouldThrow assertions inside named helpers.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split into per-scenario classes; rename Assert* helpers to neutral names (e.g. WarmCacheNotModifiedScenario) that signal they perform multi-step orchestration, not just an assertion.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: Hidden assertions at lines 349 (AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync), 391 (AssertExpectedCountETagContract), 496 (AssertPersistedEventEvidence), 508 (AssertStrongSelfRoutingETag)

high_severity_violations.description: File is 771 LOC (2.6x limit) with 32 [Fact]/[Theory] methods; representative of unsplit controller test files across the suite.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split by HTTP verb / endpoint: QueriesController_GetTests, QueriesController_PostTests, QueriesController_AuthorizationTests.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: 771 LOC, 32 test methods

high_severity_violations.description: File is 753 LOC (2.5x limit). Has a `// Story 11-3 Task 6:` story-number comment at line 29 that will rot relative to commit history per CLAUDE.md guidance.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split by orchestration concern; remove story-number annotations.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: 753 LOC + story-number rot at line 29

high_severity_violations.description: File is 709 LOC (2.4x limit). Final entry in the >700 LOC DoD-breaching cohort.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split by cache scenario (hit, miss, eviction, invalidation).
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: 709 LOC

high_severity_violations.description: An additional 80 test files exceed the 300 LOC DoD limit beyond the top 10 itemised above — total 90 violating files across the suite (632 test files total, ~14% violate DoD). The pattern is systemic, not isolated. Examples include AuthorizationBehaviorTests (676), DeadLettersPageTests (666), EventStoreSignalRClientTests (648), AccessControlPolicyTests (640), DeadLetterTraceChainTests (631), AssemblyScannerTests (621), TenantsPageTests (608), EventPublisherTests (601), AggregateActorDomainResultTests (583), DaprTestContainerFixture (562), PersistThenPublishResilienceTests (560), NamingConventionEngineTests (559).
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Treat the 300 LOC limit as a hard pre-merge check, not advisory. Add a CI guard that fails when any file under tests/ or Hexalith.Tenants/tests/ exceeds 300 LOC.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: 90 of 632 test files (~14%) violate the 300 LOC DoD; cohort spans 309-1455 LOC

high_severity_violations.description: The pattern `typeof(Actor).GetProperty("StateManager", BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance)` followed by `prop?.SetValue(actor, stateManager)` is duplicated across 24 test files (32 occurrences) despite an existing AggregateActorTestHelper that already encapsulates the pattern at line 75. The helper exists; tests do not call it.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Extract a public static `ActorTestHarness.AttachStateManager<TActor>(TActor actor, IActorStateManager mock)` and migrate all 32 sites. Estimated ~96 LOC eliminated (3 LOC per site × 32 sites) and a single point of failure if Dapr renames the property.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: Files: Telemetry/EndToEndTraceTests.cs:52; Observability/DeadLetterTraceChainTests.cs:77,138,191; Observability/DeadLetterOriginTracingTests.cs:81; Observability/DeadLetterMessageCompletenessTests.cs:72; Security/TenantInjectionPreventionTests.cs:74,112,152; Events/AtLeastOnceDeliveryTests.cs:268; Security/SecurityAuditLoggingTests.cs:142; Events/PersistThenPublishResilienceTests.cs:66; Integration/ETagActorIntegrationTests.cs:328; Security/DataPathIsolationTests.cs:144,203,254; Actors/StateMachineIntegrationTests.cs:60; Actors/MultiTenantPublicationTests.cs:55,127,169,217; Actors/EventReplayProjectionActorTests.cs:37; Actors/EventPublicationIntegrationTests.cs:57; Actors/EventDrainRecoveryTests.cs:43,63; Actors/ETagActorTests.cs:35; Actors/DeadLetterRoutingTests.cs:66; Actors/BackpressureTests.cs:58; Actors/AggregateActorGetEventsTests.cs:49

high_severity_violations.description: Five private reflection helpers (InvokeProjectionChanged at 587, InvokeOnReconnectedAsync at 595, InvokeOnClosedAsync at 606, plus the ReconnectConfigurator nested-type accessor at 620 and the private constructor invocation at 626) reach into private members of EventStoreSignalRClient. The repeated GetMethod(..., BindingFlags.NonPublic) pattern indicates the SUT exposes too little public API for testability.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Refactor EventStoreSignalRClient to expose protected/internal hooks (e.g. via [InternalsVisibleTo] or a TestHook seam) so tests do not need 5 reflection sites.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: Lines 587-635: 5 separate reflection accessors for OnProjectionChanged, OnReconnectedAsync, OnClosedAsync, ReconnectConfigurator nested type, and a private constructor

high_severity_violations.description: Helpers named `Assert*` perform multi-step orchestration that includes assertions but also state mutations and request issuance. Naming a helper Assert* implies a single-purpose check; readers expecting that surface are misled. Same anti-pattern present in another 14 files (DaprTestContainerFixture, AccessControlPolicyTests, PayloadProtectionTests, SignalRRedisBackplaneRuntimeProofTests, ValidProjectionRoundTripE2ETests, OpenApiE2ETests, HttpStaleETagProofE2ETests, ResiliencyConfigurationTests, ProblemTypeUriComplianceTests, EventDrainRecoveryTests, PubSubTopicIsolationEnforcementTests, TenantsDaprTestFixture, AspireTopologyFixture, TenantConformanceTests).
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Rename helpers to action-oriented verbs (e.g. AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync -> EnsureWarmRequestReturnsNotModifiedAndCaptureETag); keep pure assertions narrowly scoped.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: private async Task AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync(QueryIdentity query, string baselineETag, string firstCorrelationId, ...) — issues HTTP requests, captures ETags, and asserts; not a pure check

high_severity_violations.description: TenantAggregateTests uses `snake_case_with_underscores_throughout` style (e.g. `CreateTenant_with_no_prior_state_produces_TenantCreated`) — 59 of 81 test methods follow the underscore-rich `Method_with_scenario_words_produces_outcome` style instead of the codebase-standard `Method_Scenario_Expected` Pascal-style scenario seen in EventStoreAggregateTests.cs (45/50 matching) and ProjectionCheckpointTrackerTests.cs (21/22 matching). Reviewer-team consistency suffers.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Choose one convention project-wide (Method_Scenario_Expected matches the .NET community norm) and refactor TenantAggregateTests to match. Document the convention in CLAUDE.md.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: Sample naming in TenantAggregateTests (snake-style): CreateTenant_with_no_prior_state_produces_TenantCreated, AddUserToTenant_when_user_already_member_produces_UserAlreadyInTenantRejection, ChangeUserRole_with_undefined_role_produces_RoleEscalationRejection. Sample in EventStoreAggregateTests (Pascal-scenario): ProcessAsync_MatchingHandleMethod_DispatchesCorrectly, ProcessAsync_NullState_PassesNullToHandle

high_severity_violations.description: 12 test methods exceed the 80 LOC threshold across the suite. Worst offender: QueryCacheTopology_WhenProjectionChanges_InvalidatesOldETagAndCachesNewETag at 121 LOC. Other notable offenders: PostCommands_SignedHappyPath_ReachesCompletedWithEvent (112), RedisBackplane_TwoEventStoreInstances_DeliversProjectionChangedAcrossInstances (110), ProcessCommand_CrashAtEventsStored_Resume_DoesNotRePersistEvents (102), TrackIdentityAsync_MixedAndLowerCaseInputsForSameAggregate_ConvergeOnSamePersistedIdentity (104), TrackIdentityAsync_FullIdentityPage_AppendsToNextPageAndUpdatesIndex (94), TenantBootstrap_FirstSixtySeconds_NoFailureEvents (96), TrackIdentityAsync_RetryAfterPriorFailure_CompletesRegistrationOnSecondCall (89), TrackIdentityAsync_AggregateIdentityNormalization_FlowsCanonicalLowercaseToTrackerPersistence (83), StateMachineIntegrationTests:ProcessCommand_CrashAtEventsStored_Resume_PreservesResultPayload (86).
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Extract Arrange-heavy logic into private builders or shared fixtures; aim to keep test bodies under 40 LOC.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: Single-Fact 121-LOC method; readability and review cost grows superlinearly with length

high_severity_violations.description: Tier 1 unit test sleeps Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17)) to wait for hosted-service initial-delay timer; persistent regression since 2026-04-18 review.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Refactor DaprHealthHistoryCollector to accept an injectable TimeProvider (or ISystemClock / Func<TimeSpan,Task> delay) so tests can advance virtual time via FakeTimeProvider — eliminate the 17s wall-clock wait entirely.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: await collector.StartAsync(cts.Token); await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17)); // 15s delay + 2s buffer

high_severity_violations.description: Second 17-second Task.Delay in same Tier 1 file (ExecuteAsync_SkipsWrite_WhenNoComponentsReturned).
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Same TimeProvider injection as line 71 — or convert to a deterministic CancellationToken-driven trigger inside the collector that the test can fire synchronously.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17));

high_severity_violations.description: Third 17-second Task.Delay in same Tier 1 file (ExecuteAsync_ContinuesOnWriteFailure). Combined cost: 3 x 17s = 51s of CI time per Tier 1 run for one collector class.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: All three tests should share a TimeProvider abstraction; use Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider to advance time without sleeping.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(17));

determinism: Inject TimeProvider into DaprHealthHistoryCollector and rewrite all four DaprHealthHistoryCollectorTests to use Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider with explicit Advance(TimeSpan) calls; this single change eliminates 4 of 11 violations and removes 51 seconds of wall-clock wait per test run (PERSISTENT REGRESSION since 2026-04-18).

Convert PubSubDeliveryProofTests.TryWriteFaultFile / TryDeleteFaultFile to async (TryWriteFaultFileAsync) and use await Task.Delay(50) inside the bounded retry loop to remove the two synchronous Thread.Sleep calls.

Replace the bare Task.Delay(2000) sidecar-warmup waits in DaprTestContainerFixture.cs:104 and TenantsDaprTestFixture.cs:101 with a bounded poll against /v1.0/metadata or the actor placement readiness endpoint, capping at the existing HealthTimeoutSeconds budget.

Inject TimeProvider into IdempotencyRecord and the dead-letter recorder, then rewrite the three wall-clock assertions in IdempotencyRecordTests and DeadLetterMessageCompletenessTests to use FakeTimeProvider.GetUtcNow() and ShouldBe equality rather than inequality windows.

Add an .editorconfig rule (dotnet_diagnostic.HEXA_TEST_NoThreadSleep.severity = error) and a banned-API analyzer entry to fail the build when Thread.Sleep or Task.Delay >= 1s appears in tests/ outside designated polling helpers, preventing regression of the persistent DaprHealthHistoryCollector pattern.
isolation: Adopt AspirePubSubProofTestFixture's SnapshotAndSet/RestoreEnvironmentSnapshot helper as a shared TestEnvScope utility, and refactor every IAsyncLifetime fixture that mutates env vars (AspireContractTestFixture, AspireProjectionFaultTestFixture, KeycloakAuthFixture, AspireTopologyFixture, DaprTestContainerFixture, TenantsDaprTestFixture) to wrap InitializeAsync in try/catch with restore-on-failure to close the xUnit-skips-DisposeAsync hazard.

Add a Reset() method to FakeEventPublisher/FakeDeadLetterPublisher/InMemoryCommandStatusStore in src/Hexalith.EventStore.Testing/Fakes/ and call it from DaprTestContainerFixture between tests (e.g., expose a fixture.ResetCollectedState() that test class constructors invoke). This converts the current implicit isolation-via-unique-IDs into explicit isolation.

Move the static Redis ConnectionMultiplexer in EventPersistenceIntegrationTests.cs into DaprTestContainerFixture (or a shared Tier 2 RedisFixture) so it is created and disposed deterministically, and add a Tier 2 setup hook that flushes the EventStore-related Redis keys at fixture init (not full FLUSHDB which would clobber other suites).

Mirror GlobalOptionsBindingProfileTests' env-var snapshot pattern in GlobalOptionsTests (and any future env-var-mutating unit tests) so previous values are preserved across the test process. Consider a small EnvSnapshot helper in Hexalith.EventStore.Testing to make this trivial.

Add /// <summary> XML doc comments on every [CollectionDefinition], especially those with DisableParallelization = true, explaining why parallelism is disabled and what shared resource is being protected (Redis backplane, port range, AppDomain cache, etc.). This makes future maintainers' intent-preservation cheap.
maintainability: Add a hard CI guard (e.g. a roslyn analyzer or a simple PowerShell wc -l pre-merge check) that fails the build when any file under tests/ exceeds 300 LOC. 90 files (~14% of the suite) are out of compliance and the trend is worsening (EventStoreAggregateTests grew 79 LOC since 2026-04-18). Prioritise splitting the 11 oversized files >700 LOC first; they account for ~9,300 LOC of the violation budget.

Build a public ActorTestHarness helper class (e.g. tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Testing/Harness/ActorTestHarness.cs) exposing AttachStateManager<TActor>, CreateActorWithDefaults<TActor>, and a TraceContextScope. Migrate all 32 StateManager-reflection sites and the 5 SignalR private-member sites. Estimated reduction: ~150 LOC across 25 files plus a single point of failure when Dapr SDK changes the property name.

Strip Story/Epic identifiers from 164 inline test comments across 88 files. Keep WHY the test exists, drop the WHO (sprint reference). Add a CLAUDE.md rule banning sprint identifiers in source files (they belong in commit messages and the issue tracker).
performance: Highest leverage: refactor DaprHealthHistoryCollector to accept TimeProvider (Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider in tests) and remove the 3 x 17s real-time waits — recovers 51s per Tier 1 CI run and eliminates a persistent regression flagged by two prior reviews.

Convert the 9 IntegrationTests/EventStore/*Tests classes from IClassFixture<JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory> to a shared [CollectionDefinition]/ICollectionFixture pattern — recovers ~10-15s per Tier 3 run by avoiding 8 redundant WebApplicationFactory boots.

Apply [Theory]/[InlineData] consolidation to AccessControlPolicyTests (Local* / Production* mirrors) and ProjectionCheckpointTrackerTests — reduces xUnit per-test overhead and improves maintainability without changing semantics.
